module_signals.finding: Evidence quality is high: FQ2 FY25 financials, full-cycle history, mix, and balance sheet all trace to primary filings (FQ2 release S1 + 10-Q S4, FQ1 release S2, FY24 10-K S3); as-of price $94.72 verified and now two-source-corroborated (statmuse $94.37, ~8% post-print drop). The binding uncertainties are NOT evidentiary but (a) FORWARD (HBM durability, next-quarter ASP/margin direction) and (b) a disclosed GAP (no independent dated DRAM/HBM price index in-pack; industry-pricing direction rests on Micron's own ASP commentary = a lead, not proof) and (c) MODELED (normalized through-cycle EPS ~$2.7-4.5 is an estimate, not disclosed). Per harness these become a SIZE HAIRCUT, not a WATCH cap. Confidence high on the reported facts; the gaps justify a haircut, not a veto.

Theme: AI is creating a NEW scarce high-margin memory bottleneck (HBM / leading-node data-center DRAM) on top of a commodity-memory cycle that is itself on a confirmed up-leg, and Micron is 1 of only 3 suppliers. Dated and real: HBM revenue crossed $1B/qtr, data-center DRAM hit a record, DC revenue tripled YoY and surpassed 50% of total (FQ1). The ~8% drop on 2025-03-21 is a sentiment dislocation against a financially improving company - the market over-weights the QoQ revenue dip + guided GM step-down (mix/consumer softness) and under-weights HBM as a scarce, contracted, higher-margin pool re-weighting the mix. Net mildly positive: a genuine cyclical-inflection-with-secular-overlay opportunity, NOT a structural-decline trap (pool growing, balance sheet survives). The one metric that resolves the bull/bear split: HBM + data-center DRAM revenue and gross-margin trajectory.

The pool is BIFURCATED and must be split. Half A (HBM + leading-node data-center DRAM = the AI bottleneck): >$1B/qtr, growing, scarce, capacity-allocated, contracted, technically gated (advanced packaging + leading-edge yield), 3-player oligopoly - genuinely higher-quality than commodity memory and the ONLY thing that can raise Micron's structural ceiling; but durability across a FULL cycle is unproven vs a formidable leader. Half B (commodity DRAM ~70% / NAND ~29%): oligopoly but fundamentally cyclical/spot-exposed (FY22 peak -> FY23 -$5.8B loss -> FY24 recovery), low durability, NOT eroding (cyclical, not structural). Blended = a high-quality unproven-durable AI pool growing on a low-durability commodity base; mix shifting the right way. Operator/team life-arc grade 4/5 (per operator_background.md): founder-grade memory-industry CEO Mehrotra (~8 yrs + ~28-yr SanDisk co-founder track), cohesive imported core (Sadana P&L / Bhatia ops) + deep lifer technologist (DeBoer 29 yrs, owns HBM/DRAM roadmap), clean one-share-one-vote governance, team ALREADY cycle-tested (FY23 trough -> FY25 recovery). KEY INTERACTION: the 4/5 grade RAISES conviction WITHIN the cyclical band but per METHODOLOGY does NOT lift the structural ceiling above the cyclical Confirmed tier (~10-15%) - operator quality does not convert commodity memory into a 5-10yr secular compounder; only durable HBM differentiation could, and that is the open thesis question. Net +1, not +2: real growing higher-quality pool + strong team, but majority-commodity and HBM-durability-unproven.

Accounting CONFIRMS the up-leg with NO fraudulent trailing-earnings trap, but the CYCLE POSITION is the central caveat. Reported: FQ2 rev $8,053M (+38% YoY, -8% QoQ), GAAP GM 36.8% (from 18.5% YoY), non-GAAP EPS $1.56 (beat), OCF $3.94B, net capex $3.085B, adj FCF $857M (thin), cash+inv $9.60B vs debt $14.36B (net debt ~$4.76B), equity $48.6B - survivable, no bankruptcy risk. FQ3 guided to RECORD revenue $8.80B but a further non-GAAP GM step-down to 36.5%. CYCLE: FY22 $8.7B NI (peak) -> FY23 -$5.8B loss (trough, $1.8B NRV write-down) -> FY24 $0.78B -> FY25 run-rate ~$6.2 non-GAAP EPS = a confirmed MID-CYCLE UP-LEG, not trough, not yet peak. NORMALIZATION (the discipline): the ~15x run-rate multiple is on UP-LEG earnings; on modeled normalized EPS ~$3.5 the stock is ~27x = NOT cheap; P/B 2.17x is mid-cycle. HBM/data-center mix MAY raise the through-cycle margin floor structurally (legit) but the magnitude is unproven across a cycle. Watch-items: capex ~= OCF (FCF turns negative in a down-cycle); inventory $9,007M elevated (early-glut tell if it rises into softening ASPs); QoQ dip + GM step-down = first soft signals. +1 not +2: real cash-backed recovery, but size off NORMALIZED economics, not the flattering run-rate.

NO HARD VETO. The main risk is a PARTIAL false_cheap_value_trap (the up-cycle-EPS trap): at ~15x run-rate the stock looks cheap, but that EPS is an up-leg number (~27x on normalized ~$3.5); the QoQ revenue dip + guided GM step-down + named consumer softness are the early signals that the commodity ASP tailwind is maturing, and IF the cycle rolls while capex stays ~$3B/qtr the result is a glut (ASPs fall, utilization drops, FCF negative). This is real and is why size is CAPPED. BUT it is NOT a full trap: structural_decline_trap is ABSENT (pool growing not eroding, gaining DC mix, moat intact/strengthening on HBM - unlike INTC-DCG); yield_or_balance_sheet_trap is ABSENT (cash $9.60B vs debt $14.36B, equity $48.6B, FY23 -$5.8B loss absorbed without distress, tiny dividend - no bankruptcy path). Hard-veto conditions (moat erosion / normalized FAR below trailing AND structurally impaired / capex destroying owner earnings on a DECLINING pool / permanent impairment) are NOT met - the impairment here is CYCLICAL (recoverable), not STRUCTURAL (permanent). Strongest bear case (credible, caps size, but survivable): you pay a full normalized multiple on up-leg earnings hoping HBM permanently lifts through-cycle margin; if the cycle rolls and HBM normalizes you lose 40-60% to normalized fair value - a drawdown-to-fair-value risk, not a zero. So M5 = size cap, not kill.

At $94.72 (~$105.6B mkt cap, ~$110B EV) MU trades ~15x up-leg run-rate non-GAAP EPS / ~27x modeled normalized EPS (~$3.5) / ~2.17x book, after an ~8% post-print drop. For a CYCLICAL on a confirmed up-leg, the cyclical_inflection rule says price off NORMALIZED mid-cycle economics - on which this is FAIR, not cheap (you are not getting a trough entry near ~1x book). The mild positive is the dislocation (~8% drop on a mix blip, not a thesis break) + favorable asymmetry at small size: survivable downside (fortress-enough balance sheet, no bankruptcy path, recoverable cyclical drawdown) against the HBM/AI-memory optionality, at a book multiple that is mid-range not stretched. This supports a STARTER now with room to add into weakness or on confirmation; it does NOT support paying up aggressively or full-sizing, because the entry is mid-cycle (not trough) and on a full normalized multiple. Not a fat pitch; a sensible asymmetric starter.

starter_zone: <= ~$95 (at/around the as-of $94.72 post-drop level; the ~8% dislocation gives a sensible starter entry on normalized-fair economics)
add_zone: $70-$85 on cyclical/market weakness (closer to ~1.6-1.8x book, a better normalized entry), OR at any price once a forward confirmation lands: HBM + data-center DRAM revenue keeps growing AND blended gross margin holds/re-expands (proving the GM step-down was a mix blip, not a cycle top) AND inventory does not balloon into softening ASPs. Add toward the ~10-15% Confirmed tier on that confirmation - a cyclical caps below Core.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: No-chase well above normalized fair value (paying up for up-leg earnings as if permanent - e.g. a multiple that only works on peak EPS). Trim only on the four methodology triggers (thesis wrong / absurd price vs normalized value / clearly better idea / thesis disconfirmed). A QoQ dip or high weight alone is NOT a trim reason.
binding_constraint: M4/M5 cycle-position + M3 HBM-durability: you are buying a majority-commodity cyclical on a confirmed MID-CYCLE UP-LEG at a FULL normalized multiple (~27x on ~$3.5 normalized EPS; the ~15x run-rate is on flattered up-leg earnings), on the unproven thesis that HBM/AI-memory permanently raises the through-cycle margin floor. The QoQ revenue dip + guided GM step-down + named consumer softness are early signals the commodity ASP tailwind may be maturing. This is what holds the position at STARTER and caps it at the ~10-15% Confirmed tier (NOT Core): the 4/5 operator team and growing HBM pool lift conviction WITHIN the cyclical band, but conviction/size into the ceiling must be EARNED by HBM differentiation + blended margin durability proving out (and the GM step-down NOT extending into a cycle top). A size + cycle-timing constraint, not a veto.
kill_criteria: Cyclical rollover confirmed: data-center/HBM demand softens OR commodity AND HBM ASPs decline together, gross margin steps down FURTHER beyond the FQ3 guide, while capex stays ~$3B/qtr -> glut; cut back toward tracking.

HBM differentiation fails: HBM share/qualification slips vs the leader, or HBM pricing normalizes fast as all three players flood capacity -> the Half-A premium evaporates and MU re-commoditizes; cut back.
Capex destroys FCF: sustained ~$3B/qtr capex into a down-cycle with FCF deeply negative and balance-sheet stress -> the multi-year build was mis-timed; reassess.
Inventory glut signal: inventory keeps rising into softening ASPs (the early glut tell) -> trim / do not add.

Normalized-earnings thesis breaks: evidence the HBM mix does NOT structurally lift the through-cycle margin floor (blended margin reverts to prior-cycle levels) -> the full normalized multiple paid is unjustified; cut back.
runner_dissent: Mild dissent on ENTRY TIMING / size, not direction. The mechanical read parks this at STARTER 4% with a 12% Confirmed-tier ceiling, which I endorse as correct for a cyclical entered mid-up-leg at a full normalized multiple. The genuine tension the framework handles well here (vs the old framework that would either chase the ~15x 'cheap' run-rate multiple OR reject a cyclical outright): this is a real asymmetric starter - a strong 4/5 cycle-tested team capturing a growing, scarce HBM pool, with survivable downside - so REJECT would be wrong; but it is NOT trough-cheap and the up-leg + GM-step-down argue against full-sizing, so CORE would also be wrong. One residual caution I want explicit: a better normalized entry sits lower (~$70-85, closer to 1.6-1.8x book); a disciplined cyclical investor could legitimately run this as a 1-2% tracking sliver here and wait for either a cheaper price or a confirmation that the GM step-down was a mix blip, rather than a full 4% starter at a mid-cycle price. I hold the 4% starter because the ~8% dislocation + the HBM optionality + survivable downside make a real (not tracking) initial position defensible, with the explicit discipline to add only on confirmation and to size off NORMALIZED, not run-rate, economics.
